properties.name: productKey
properties.description:
Your product key from the Pillar app.
Get it at app.trypillar.com

properties.name: platform
properties.description:
Platform identifier for code-first actions.
Used to filter actions by deployment platform.

properties.name: version
properties.description:
App version for code-first actions.
Used to match actions to the correct deployment version.

properties.name: debug
properties.description:
Enable debug mode for verbose logging and debug panel.
When enabled:
- All SDK events are logged to console
- A debug panel shows real-time execution flow
- Network requests/responses are captured

properties.name: panel
properties.description: Panel layout and behavior settings.

properties.name: edgeTrigger
properties.description: Edge trigger (sidebar tab that opens the panel).

properties.name: mobileTrigger
properties.description: Mobile trigger (floating button on small screens).

properties.name: urlParams
properties.description: URL params for auto-opening the panel.

properties.name: textSelection
properties.description: Text selection "Ask AI" popover.

properties.name: domScanning
properties.description: DOM scanning for page context.

properties.name: suggestions
properties.description: Page-aware suggestions.

properties.name: sidebarTabs
properties.description: Sidebar tabs configuration.

properties.name: apiBaseUrl
properties.description: API base URL. Defaults to Pillar's production API.

properties.name: theme
properties.description: Theme customization.

properties.name: customCSS
properties.description:
Custom CSS to inject into the panel's Shadow DOM.
Use public class names (pillar-*) to override default styles.

properties.name: onReady
properties.description: Called when the SDK is initialized and ready.

properties.name: onError
properties.description: Called when the SDK encounters an error.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether the panel is enabled.

properties.name: position
properties.description: Which side of the screen the panel appears on.

properties.name: mode
properties.description: Panel mode: 'overlay' slides over content, 'push' shifts content aside.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Panel width in pixels.

properties.name: container
properties.description:
Custom mount point for the panel.
- CSS selector string (e.g., '#pillar-panel')
- HTMLElement reference
- 'manual' for React component-based mounting
- undefined (default) mounts to document.body

properties.name: useShadowDOM
properties.description:
Whether to use Shadow DOM for style isolation.
- false (default): Panel renders in regular DOM, inherits host app CSS.
  Custom cards can use the host app's design system (Tailwind, etc.)
- true: Panel renders in Shadow DOM, fully isolated from host CSS.
  Use this if you need style isolation on third-party sites.

properties.name: hoverBreakpoint
properties.description:
Viewport width below which the panel switches from 'push' mode to 'hover' mode.
In hover mode, the panel floats over content instead of pushing it aside.
- number: The breakpoint in pixels (default: 1200)
- false: Disable responsive behavior, always use push mode

properties.name: hoverBackdrop
properties.description:
Whether to show a backdrop overlay when the panel is in hover mode.
Only applies when viewport is below hoverBreakpoint.

properties.name: fullWidthBreakpoint
properties.description: Viewport width below which the panel takes full screen width.

properties.name: initialOpen
properties.description:
Whether to open the panel automatically on initialization.
Takes priority over localStorage persisted state.

ThemeConfig.name: ThemeConfig
ThemeConfig.description: Theme configuration for customizing panel appearance

properties.name: mode
properties.description: Color mode: 'light', 'dark', or 'auto' (follows system preference)

properties.name: colors
properties.description: Custom color overrides for light mode

properties.name: darkColors
properties.description: Custom color overrides for dark mode (when mode is 'auto' or 'dark')

properties.name: fontFamily
properties.default: "-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif"
properties.description: Font family for all panel text.

ThemeColors.name: ThemeColors
ThemeColors.description:
Theme color configuration
All colors should be valid CSS color values (hex, rgb, hsl, etc.)

properties.name: primary
properties.description: Primary brand color (buttons, links, accents)

properties.name: primaryHover
properties.description: Primary color on hover state

properties.name: background
properties.description: Main background color

properties.name: backgroundSecondary
properties.description: Secondary/subtle background (inputs, cards)

properties.name: text
properties.description: Primary text color

properties.name: textMuted
properties.description: Muted/secondary text color

properties.name: border
properties.description: Border color

properties.name: borderLight
properties.description: Border color for subtle/light borders

properties.name: outlineColor
properties.description: Outline/focus ring color for interactive elements

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Whether to show the edge trigger sidebar tab.
When enabled, a slim vertical tab appears on the screen edge that opens the panel.

properties.name: resizable
properties.description:
Whether the panel can be resized by dragging the edge of the sidebar trigger.
When enabled, a drag handle appears on the inner edge of the sidebar when the panel is open.
The resized width is persisted to localStorage.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Whether to show the mobile floating button on small screens.
When enabled, a floating action button appears when viewport is below mobileBreakpoint.

properties.name: breakpoint
properties.description: Viewport width below which the edge trigger hides and mobile trigger shows.

properties.name: position
properties.description: Position of the floating button.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: Preset icon to display in the button.

properties.name: customIcon
properties.description: Custom SVG icon string. Overrides the icon preset if provided.

properties.name: backgroundColor
properties.description:
Background color of the button (CSS value).
Defaults to the theme's primary color.

properties.name: iconColor
properties.description: Icon color (CSS value).

properties.name: size
properties.description:
Button size - preset name or pixel value.
- 'small': 44px
- 'medium': 56px (default)
- 'large': 68px

properties.name: label
properties.description: Tooltip text and aria-label for accessibility.

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Offset from screen edges in pixels.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether to check URL params for auto-opening the panel (default: true)

properties.name: prefix
properties.description: Prefix for URL params (default: 'pillar-')

properties.name: clearAfterOpen
properties.description: Whether to clear URL params after opening (default: true)

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether to show "Ask AI" popover on text selection (default: true)

properties.name: label
properties.description: Label for the popover button (default: 'Ask AI')

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Whether DOM scanning is enabled.
When enabled, page structure is captured and sent with messages.

properties.name: includeText
properties.description: Whether to include text content in the scan.

properties.name: maxDepth
properties.description: Maximum depth to traverse the DOM tree.

properties.name: visibleOnly
properties.description: Whether to only include visible elements.

properties.name: excludeSelector
properties.description: CSS selector for elements to exclude from scanning.

properties.name: maxTextLength
properties.description: Maximum text length before truncation.

properties.name: interactionHighlight
properties.description: Configuration for highlighting elements during AI interactions.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Whether to highlight elements when the AI interacts with them.

properties.name: outlineColor
properties.description: Outline color for highlighted elements (CSS color value).

properties.name: outlineWidth
properties.description: Outline width in pixels.

properties.name: outlineOffset
properties.description: Outline offset in pixels (space between element and outline).

properties.name: duration
properties.description:
Duration in milliseconds to show the highlight.
Set to 0 for no auto-removal (highlight stays until next interaction).

properties.name: scrollIntoView
properties.description: Whether to scroll the element into view if not visible.

properties.name: scrollBehavior
properties.description: Scroll behavior when scrolling element into view.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description:
Enable page-aware suggestion sorting.
When enabled, suggestions are re-sorted based on the current page context
whenever the user navigates to a new route.

properties.name: debounceMs
properties.description:
Debounce time for route change detection (ms).
Prevents excessive sorting on rapid navigation.

properties.name: displayLimit
properties.description: Maximum number of suggestions to display.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: Preset icon for this tab

Context.name: Context
Context.description:
Context for the assistant.
Pass this to help the assistant understand what the user is doing.

properties.name: currentPage
properties.description: Current page path (e.g., "/settings/billing")

properties.name: currentFeature
properties.description: Current feature or section (e.g., "Billing Settings")

properties.name: userRole
properties.description: User's role in the product (e.g., "admin", "member")

properties.name: userState
properties.description: Current user state or mode (e.g., "onboarding", "trial")

properties.name: errorState
properties.description: Any error state the user is experiencing

properties.name: custom
properties.description: Custom context data

properties.name: userId
properties.description: User identifier (for conversation continuity)

properties.name: name
properties.description: User's name for personalized responses

properties.name: role
properties.description: User's role/permissions

properties.name: accountType
properties.description: Account/organization info

properties.name: experienceLevel
properties.description: User's experience level

Suggestion.name: Suggestion
Suggestion.description: Suggestion returned from the suggestions API.

TaskExecutePayload.name: TaskExecutePayload
TaskExecutePayload.description: Task execution payload - sent when a task button is clicked.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Database UUID for the task (used for confirmation)

properties.name: name
properties.description: Task unique identifier (e.g., 'invite_team_member')

properties.name: data
properties.description: Task data payload

properties.name: taskType
properties.description: Task type hint

properties.name: path
properties.description: Path template for navigate type (already resolved with params)

properties.name: externalUrl
properties.description: External URL for external_link type

CardCallbacks.name: CardCallbacks
CardCallbacks.description: Callbacks provided to custom card renderers.

properties.name: onConfirm
properties.description: Called when user confirms the action. Pass modified data if needed.

properties.name: onCancel
properties.description: Called when user cancels the action

properties.name: onStateChange
properties.description: Called to report card state changes (for analytics/confirmation)

CardFieldSchema.name: CardFieldSchema
CardFieldSchema.description: Card field schema definition for declarative card configuration.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Field name/key

properties.name: label
properties.description: Display label

properties.name: type
properties.description: Field type

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether field is required

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: Default value

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: Placeholder text

properties.name: helpText
properties.description: Help text shown below field

properties.name: options
properties.description: Options for select/multiselect types

properties.name: validation
properties.description: Validation rules

properties.name: cardType
properties.description: Card type identifier (e.g., 'add_to_deal', 'invite_member')

properties.name: title
properties.description: Human-readable card title

properties.name: description
properties.description: Card description

properties.name: schema
properties.description: Schema defining form fields (for auto-generated forms)

properties.name: renderer
properties.description: Custom renderer function (overrides schema-based rendering)

properties.name: theme
properties.description: Theme/styling options

PanelPosition.name: PanelPosition
PanelPosition.description: Which side of the screen the panel appears on.

PanelMode.name: PanelMode
PanelMode.description: How the panel interacts with page content: 'overlay' floats over it, 'push' shifts it aside.

ThemeMode.name: ThemeMode
ThemeMode.description: Color mode for the panel theme. 'auto' follows the system preference.

MobileTriggerPosition.name: MobileTriggerPosition
MobileTriggerPosition.description: Position of the mobile floating trigger button.

MobileTriggerIcon.name: MobileTriggerIcon
MobileTriggerIcon.description: Preset icon for the mobile floating trigger button.

MobileTriggerSize.name: MobileTriggerSize
MobileTriggerSize.description: Size preset for the mobile floating trigger button.

CardRenderer.name: CardRenderer
CardRenderer.description:
Card renderer function signature.
Customers register these to render custom confirmation cards.
CardRenderer.type:
(
  container: HTMLElement,
  data: Record<string, unknown>,
  callbacks: CardCallbacks
) => (() => void) | void

methods.name: init
methods.description: Initialize the Pillar SDK

methods.name: getInstance
methods.description: Get the current Pillar instance

methods.name: destroy
methods.description: Destroy the Pillar instance

methods.name: getDebugLog
methods.description:
Get debug log entries (for debug panel).
Returns empty array if debug mode is not enabled.

methods.name: onDebugLog
methods.description:
Subscribe to debug log updates (for debug panel).
Returns unsubscribe function.

methods.name: clearDebugLog
methods.description: Clear debug log entries.

methods.name: on
methods.signature: on(event: K, callback: (data: PillarEvents[K]) => void): () => void
methods.description: Subscribe to SDK events

methods.name: open
methods.description: Open the help panel

parameters.name: options

methods.name: close
methods.description: Close the help panel

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Toggle the help panel

methods.name: navigate
methods.signature: navigate(view: string, params?: Record<string, string>): void
methods.description: Navigate to a specific view in the panel

methods.name: setContext
methods.description:
Set context for the assistant.
Use this to tell Pillar what the user is doing for smarter, more relevant assistance.

parameters.name: ctx
parameters.description: - Context fields to set (merges with existing context)

methods.name: getChatContext
methods.description:
Get the current chat context (conversation ID and messages).
Useful for escalation to human support with conversation history.

methods.name: setTheme
methods.description:
Update the theme at runtime.
Use this to sync with your app's theme (e.g., dark mode toggle).

parameters.name: theme
parameters.description: - Partial theme config to merge with current theme

methods.name: setTextSelectionEnabled
methods.description: Enable or disable the text selection "Ask AI" popover at runtime.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: - Whether to show the popover when text is selected

methods.name: setDOMScanningEnabled
methods.description: Enable or disable DOM scanning at runtime.

methods.name: getElement
methods.description: Get a DOM element by its pillar selector.

methods.name: clickElement
methods.description:
Click an element by its selector.
Uses realistic mouse event simulation (mousedown → mouseup → click)
for better compatibility with UI frameworks.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: - CSS selector for the element to click

methods.name: typeInElement
methods.description:
Type text into an input element.
Uses realistic input simulation that works with React controlled inputs.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: - CSS selector for the input element

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: - Text to type into the element

methods.name: selectOption
methods.description:
Select an option in a select element.
Uses realistic event simulation for React compatibility.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: - CSS selector for the select element

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: - Value of the option to select

methods.name: focusElement
methods.description:
Focus an element by its selector.
Fires both focus and focusin events for compatibility.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: - CSS selector for the element to focus

methods.name: toggleElement
methods.description:
Toggle a checkbox or radio element.
Uses click simulation for realistic behavior and React compatibility.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: - CSS selector for the checkbox/radio element

methods.name: handlePageInteraction
methods.description:
Handle the interact_with_page action from the LLM.
Routes to appropriate DOM interaction method based on operation.
Validates the ref format, verifies the element attribute, and
prompts user confirmation for destructive actions.

methods.name: mountPanelTo
methods.description:
Mount the panel to a specific container element.
Used for manual mounting mode (e.g., from React component).

methods.name: getPanelHostElement
methods.description: Get the panel host element for external mounting

methods.name: setUserProfile
methods.description: Set the user profile for personalization.

methods.name: identify
methods.description:
Identify the current user after login.

Call this when a user logs into your application to:
- Link their anonymous conversation history to their account
- Enable cross-device conversation history retrieval
- Associate future conversations with their user ID

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: - Your application's user ID for this user

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: - Optional user profile data (name, email, metadata)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: - Optional settings for the identify call

methods.name: logout
methods.description:
Clear the user's identity (logout).

Call this when a user logs out of your application.
Future conversations will be tracked anonymously until identify() is called again.

Note: This does not delete existing conversations - they remain associated
with the user's account for future retrieval.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: - Optional settings for the logout call

methods.name: reportAction
methods.signature: reportAction(action: string, metadata?: Record<string, unknown>): void
methods.description:
Report a user action for context building.
Recent actions are tracked and sent with chat requests for better context.

methods.name: clearErrorState
methods.description: Clear any error state from the context.

methods.name: setErrorState
methods.description:
Set an error state in the context.
The assistant will use this to provide relevant troubleshooting help.

methods.name: getSuggestions
methods.description:
Get contextual help suggestions based on current context.
Returns relevant articles, videos, and actions.

methods.name: getAssistantContext
methods.description:
Get the full context object to send to the backend.
Used internally by the API client.

methods.name: onTask
methods.signature: onTask(taskName: string, handler: (data: Record<string, unknown>) => void): () => void
methods.description:
Register a handler for a specific task.
Called when the AI suggests a task and the user clicks it.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: - Function to handle the task execution

methods.name: registerAction
methods.signature: registerAction(action: { name: string } & Record<string, unknown>): void
methods.description:
Register an action definition at runtime.

This is primarily for demos and development. In production, actions
should be synced via the `pillar-sync` CLI during CI/CD.

The action definition is stored locally and can be used by `onTask`
handlers. For actions with `returnsData: true`, the handler's return
value is sent back to the agent.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: - Action definition with name and properties

methods.name: getRegisteredAction
methods.description: Get a registered action definition by name.

methods.name: getHandler
methods.signature: getHandler(actionName: string): ((data: Record<string, unknown>) => unknown) | undefined
methods.description:
Get handler for an action, checking all registration systems.

Lookup order:
1. Code-first action registry (synced via pillar-sync CLI) - handler in definition
2. Task handlers (registered via onTask at runtime)

This is the recommended pattern:
- Action definitions synced to server via CLI (so AI knows what's possible)
- Handlers registered at runtime via onTask (client-side execution)

methods.name: onAnyTask
methods.signature: onAnyTask(handler: (name: string, data: Record<string, unknown>) => void): () => void
methods.description:
Register a catch-all handler for any task.
Useful for logging, analytics, or handling unknown tasks.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: - Function called with task name and data

methods.name: offTask
methods.description: Remove a task handler.

parameters.name: taskName
parameters.description: - The task identifier to stop handling

methods.name: executeTask
methods.description:
Execute a task programmatically.
This is called internally by the widget when a user clicks a task button.
Can also be called directly if you want to trigger a task.

methods.name: completeTask
methods.signature: completeTask(taskName: string, success?: boolean, data?: Record<string, unknown>): void
methods.description:
Mark a task as complete.
Call this after your task handler finishes successfully.

methods.name: completeAction
methods.signature: completeAction(actionName: string, success?: boolean, data?: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Signal that an action has completed.

For simple actions, this emits the completion event.
For wizard actions (modals, multi-step flows), call this when the user
finishes the flow.

parameters.name: success
parameters.description: - Whether the action completed successfully (default: true)

methods.name: confirmTaskExecution
methods.description:
Confirm task execution result.
Call this after your task handler completes to report success/failure
back to Pillar for implementation status tracking.

parameters.name: taskId
parameters.description: - The database UUID of the task (from task:execute event)

parameters.name: details

methods.name: registerCard
methods.description:
Register a custom card renderer for inline_ui type actions.

When the AI returns an action with action_type: 'inline_ui' and
a card_type in its data, the SDK will look for a registered renderer
and call it to render the inline UI card.

parameters.name: renderer
parameters.description: - Function that renders the card into a container

methods.name: getCardRenderer
methods.description:
Get a registered card renderer by type.
Returns undefined if no renderer is registered for the given type.

methods.name: hasCardRenderer
methods.description: Check if a card renderer is registered for a given type.

methods.name: startWorkflow
methods.description:
Start a workflow.
Called when the AI returns a workflow in its response.

methods.name: initiateWorkflowStep
methods.description:
Initiate a workflow step that requires user confirmation.
Called when user clicks "Start" on a step with auto_run=false.

methods.name: confirmWorkflowStep
methods.description:
Confirm a workflow step as complete.
Called by the host app after the action is done.
Automatically advances to the next step.

methods.name: skipWorkflowStep
methods.description: Skip a workflow step.

methods.name: cancelWorkflow
methods.description: Cancel the active workflow.

methods.name: sendActionResult
methods.signature: sendActionResult(actionName: string, result: unknown, toolCallId?: string): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Send action result back to the agent.

Called automatically for actions with `returns: true` after their
handler completes. The result is sent to the agent for further reasoning.

parameters.name: actionName
parameters.description: - The name of the action that was executed

parameters.name: result
parameters.description: - The result data to send back to the agent

parameters.name: toolCallId
parameters.description: - Unique ID for this specific tool invocation (for result correlation)

methods.name: executeQueryAction
methods.signature: executeQueryAction(actionName: string, args?: Record<string, unknown>, schema?: { properties?: Record<string, unknown>; required?: string[] }): Promise<void>
methods.description:
Execute a query action and send the result back to the agent.

This is called when the agent sends a `query_request` event.
Query actions are expected to return data that the agent can use
for further reasoning.

parameters.name: actionName
parameters.description: - The name of the action to execute

methods.name: state
methods.description: Current SDK state

methods.name: isReady
methods.description: Whether SDK is ready

methods.name: isPanelOpen
methods.description: Whether the panel is currently open

methods.name: config
methods.description: Get the resolved configuration

methods.name: isDebugEnabled
methods.description: Whether debug mode is enabled

methods.name: isDOMScanningEnabled
methods.description: Whether DOM scanning is currently enabled.

methods.name: context
methods.description: Get the current context

methods.name: userProfile
methods.description: Get the current user profile

methods.name: externalUserId
methods.description: Get the current external user ID (if identified).

methods.name: isIdentified
methods.description: Whether the current user is identified (logged in).

methods.name: workflow
methods.description: Get the active workflow, if any.
